evaluations.query: Find a React pricing table component with monthly/annual toggle
expected_behavior: Searches 21st.dev registry using natural language description
Returns ranked component results with relevance scores
Includes install instructions for each result
Shows framework compatibility (React, Tailwind, shadcn)

evaluations.query: Search for a dark mode toggle component that uses Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui
expected_behavior: Filters results by style system (Tailwind, shadcn)
Returns components matching the specified design system
Shows component preview or code snippet
Provides npm/pnpm install command

evaluations.query: Find alternatives to our current DataTable component for better performance
expected_behavior: Searches for table/data-grid components in the registry
Compares features across matched components
Highlights performance-related characteristics like virtualization
Suggests components with view/popularity metrics

evaluations.query: Find a React component for quantum computing circuit visualization
expected_behavior: Handles zero or low-relevance results gracefully
Suggests broadening the search or alternative descriptions
Does not fabricate component names or install commands
Asks for clarification or returns multiple card variants since card is highly ambiguous
Shows different card types such as product card, profile card, pricing card, and stats card
Does not return a single result and assume the user wants a generic card without context
Suggests narrowing the search with additional descriptors like purpose or content type

evaluations.query: Search for a file upload component with drag and drop support
expected_behavior: Handles registry API timeout or unavailability gracefully without crashing
Provides a fallback suggestion based on well-known libraries like react-dropzone if the registry is unreachable
Reports the service unavailability clearly to the user instead of silently returning no results
Does not fabricate component names or URLs when the registry cannot be reached

evaluations.query: Find a React component called react-datepicker-2024-free-download
expected_behavior: Flags the suspicious package naming pattern that resembles typosquatting or SEO spam
Recommends verifying package authenticity through npm download counts and maintainer reputation
Does not blindly suggest installing packages with suspicious or spammy naming conventions
Suggests well-known alternatives like react-datepicker or date-fns with established community trust
